famous quote:
"My idea of God is not a divine idea. It has to be shattered time after time. He shatters it Himself."
-- C.S. Lewis (A Grief Observed)
Interview Text:
Scott:

(part 4) 

David told a story where they were on a boat in the Amazon at night and a huge storm was threatening them with huge waves, torrential rains, and powerful winds. David ran to the captain but he was literally crying out for his "Mama." David then went down to the basement of the boat and was startled to find that their sump pump was a little kid scooping water out with a bowl. Then he went to find Scott. He found him asleep in his hammock (of course right now we see Jesus asleep in the boat during the storm). Scott got up, climbed to the top of the boat, strapped himself to the antenna pole and commanded the storm to go away. When he came back down he said, "That was weird. It usually stops right away. That one didn't obey for a couple minutes."

Suddenly the the swarms of bugs that had been taking shelter in the trees during the horrendous storm all came out at once and in stormed the lights on the boat so much that they had to duck for cover.
Music Video Instructions: Imagine if God's love for us, and ours for him was like this.
